incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune endpoint management console and remotely triggered mass device wipes across the company's global network — no malware deployed.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices wiped across factories in the US, Ireland, India, and other countries; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland; employees locked out of critical systems for over a week. DOJ seized four MOIS-linked domains; FBI and CISA issued joint Intune hardening advisory.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners and DDoS targets worldwide
incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerable IoT devices (routers, cameras, DVRs, TV boxes) and abused residential proxy networks to build four botnets — Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad.
incidents.impact: Three million devices hijacked; over 300,000 DDoS attacks launched including a record 29.7 Tbps attack. DOJ seized command-and-control infrastructure with support from law enforcement in Canada, Germany, the Netherlands, and Europol.

incidents.vector: Interlock ransomware gang exploited a zero-day in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (CVE-2026-20131) weeks before public disclosure to gain initial access.
incidents.impact: Multiple critical infrastructure organizations compromised; the same gang previously crippled St. Paul, MN city government (requiring National Guard assistance), DaVita dialysis, and a major Ohio healthcare system. Amazon discovered the exploitation through a misconfigured Interlock staging server.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian website visitors and iPhone users globally
incidents.attacker: Unknown — multiple state and commercial spyware operators
incidents.vector: Watering hole attack embedding the 'Darksword' iOS exploit kit on dozens of Ukrainian websites, targeting iPhones running iOS 18.4 through 18.6.2.
incidents.impact: Second iOS exploit kit discovered this month; capable of stealing data and crypto wallet information. An estimated 220–270 million iPhones remain on vulnerable iOS versions. Google confirmed separate campaigns by multiple state-linked and commercial actors targeting Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.

incidents.vector: Phishing email exploiting a cross-site scripting flaw in Zimbra webmail (CVE-2025-66376) — entire attack payload embedded in the HTML body of a single email with no attachments or links.
incidents.impact: Attackers harvested login credentials, session tokens, two-factor authentication backup codes, browser-stored passwords, and up to 90 days of mailbox data from the maritime navigation agency.

incidents.vector: Data analyst contractor used company laptop access to exfiltrate corporate data including employee PII and compensation records, then sent 60+ extortion emails demanding ransom after his contract ended.
incidents.impact: Company paid approximately $2.5 million in ransom. Attacker convicted on six counts of extortion and faces up to 12 years in prison. Case highlights insider threat risk from contractor access.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to a warehouse management system used for Thai procurement parts logistics.
incidents.impact: Employee PII from Mazda group companies and suppliers potentially leaked externally. No customer data was affected. Mazda warned of potential phishing using the stolen email addresses.
